perturbation.original_text: COUNTRY Country as used in this Agreement shall mean country, territory or place, as appropriate. For the purposes of Paragraph 15 of this Agreement the Country associated with Telia shall mean Denmark.
perturbation.changed_text: COUNTRY Country as used in this Agreement shall mean country, territory or place, as appropriate. For the purposes of Paragraph 15 of this Agreement the Territory associated with Telia shall mean Denmark.
perturbation.explanation: The change of 'Country' to 'Territory' in relation to Telia within the definition of 'COUNTRY' introduces an inconsistency. Throughout the agreement, 'Country' is used with a specific defined meaning, and this alteration suggests a different legal or operational scope for Telia, leading to uncertainty in Paragraph 15 about the extent of Telia's obligations, due to the internal contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Terminal Parties shall use all reasonable efforts to furnish and maintain, or cause to be furnished and maintained, in working order for the other Parties and for the Purchasers in TAT-14, for the duration of this Agreement, the necessary facilities in their respective Countries as may be reasonably required for extending capacity in TAT-14 assigned to such Parties or Purchasers for the purpose of handling communications transiting the Country involved. No Party shall be required under this Agreement to furnish such transit facilities in its Country to other Parties or Purchasers of its own Country.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters 'Country' to 'Territory' in the definition of 'COUNTRY' specifically for Telia in Paragraph 15. The contradicted text refers to 'Countries' when discussing facilities and obligations. By redefining what applies to Telia in Paragraph 15 to 'Territory' instead of 'Country', the changed text introduces ambiguity about whether the obligations related to facilities in the contradicted text apply to Telia, creating an in-text contradiction because 'Country' is not consistently applied.

perturbation.original_text: MAINTENANCE AUTHORITIES The Maintenance Authorities in TAT-14 shall be the Terminal Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: MAINTENANCE AUTHORITIES The Maintenance Providers in TAT-14 shall be the Terminal Parties.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Authorities' to 'Providers' when defining 'MAINTENANCE AUTHORITIES' causes confusion. While Terminal Parties are responsible for maintenance, altering the defined term suggests that their role is merely to provide resources, not necessarily to authorize or oversee maintenance activities. This contradiction impacts sections discussing responsibilities and decision-making powers related to maintenance, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Maintenance Authorities shall have the right to temporarily de-activate Segment S, or any part thereof, in order to perform their duties as Maintenance Authorities. Prior to such de-activation, reasonable notice shall be given to and coordination shall be made with the other Parties. To the extent possible, sixty (60) days prior to initiating action, the Maintenance Authority(ies) shall advise the other Parties in writing of the timing, scope, and costs of significant planned maintenance operations, of significant changes to existing operation and maintenance methods, and of contractual arrangements for cable ships that will or may have a significant impact on operation or maintenance costs. Should one or more Parties representing at least 5 % of the total voting interests specified in Schedule B wish to review such an operation or change prior to its occurrence, such Party or Parties shall notify the Maintenance Authorities involved and the Managing Group chairperson in writing within thirty (30) days of such advice. Upon such notification, the Managing Group shall initiate action to convene an ad hoc meeting for such review.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Authorities' to 'Providers' fundamentally alters the role described. The original 'Maintenance Authorities' implies decision-making power ('right to temporarily de-activate'). The changed text, 'Maintenance Providers,' suggests a more limited role focused on supplying resources. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which grants 'Maintenance Authorities' the 'right to temporarily de-activate' and requires them to advise on significant changes, implying authority beyond simply providing maintenance. The term 'Providers' implies a lack of such authority, thereby causing contradiction. This falls under Misaligned Terminology causing in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Allocated Capacity Number of Ring-MlUs distributed to Parties, based on their financial commitments at the time of signing of this Agreement, as shown in Schedule C.
perturbation.changed_text: Allocated Capacity Number of Ring-MlUs distributed to Members, based on their financial commitments at the time of signing of this Agreement, as shown in Schedule C.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Parties' to 'Members' within the definition of 'Allocated Capacity' creates confusion about who is eligible to receive the capacity. Throughout the agreement, 'Parties' refers to the signatories, but 'Members' is undefined, potentially excluding some signatories or including non-signatories, therefore it creates an in-text contradiction. This specifically affects how allocated capacity is handled and who has rights to it, creating uncertainty about what each Party rights are.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties hereby acquire Allocated Capacity in the form of Ring-MlUs on an ownership basis as shown in Schedule C. After the signing of this Agreement, capacity may only be acquired through a Purchase Contract or through a Private Agreement, in accordance with this Paragraph 9.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Parties' to 'Members' introduces a contradiction because the agreement explicitly defines 'Parties' (the signatories) and grants them 'Allocated Capacity'. By changing this to 'Members,' which is undefined, the agreement now conflicts with the clause stating 'The Parties hereby acquire Allocated Capacity'. This creates uncertainty and potential unenforceability as it is unclear who now has the right to the allocated capacity, creating a direct conflict in who is entitled to the 'Ring-MlUs' as described in Schedule C. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
